name: Subtitle OCR
description: Converts embedded image-based subtitle tracks (VobSub/DVD and PGS/Blu-ray) to external SRT files using nOCR pattern matching. Pure managed code: no Tesseract and no native dependencies. The .nocr database format is interchange-compatible with Subtitle Edit, so databases trained or corrected in its GUI drop straight in. A scheduled task scans your Movie and Episode libraries and writes an SRT beside each image-based track.
overview: OCR image-based DVD and Blu-ray subtitles to SRT

versions.changelog: Detect image-based subtitles by probing every Movie and Episode with ffprobe, since Jellyfin does not store PGS/VobSub streams in its metadata. A per-file cache skips files that are unchanged and were probed within a configurable window, with a force-rescan option. Word spacing now scales with subtitle line height, fixing spaces inserted between every letter on HD Blu-ray text. New options: extract only selected languages, discard a track whose dropped-event ratio is too high (removing any stale SRT), and a filename tag ({name}.{lang}.OCR.srt, shown as the subtitle title) so the plugin only ever overwrites or deletes its own output. A config-page modal lists every extracted file with a link to its movie or episode. The scheduled task now sits under its own category.

Initial production release. OCRs embedded image-based subtitles (VobSub/DVD and PGS/Blu-ray) to SRT files using nOCR pattern matching, with Subtitle Edit-compatible .nocr databases. Pure managed code: no Tesseract, no native dependencies. Optionally skips image tracks whose language already has a text subtitle.
